title: Spot atom exhaustion in three sibling controllers
prompt:
The following code shows three controllers that all use `String.to_atom/1` with slightly different wrappings — an attacker can target any one of them to exhaust the atom table. Rewrite all three so none call `String.to_atom/1`, `List.to_atom/1`, or `:"..."` with interpolation:

```elixir
defmodule MyAppWeb.FilterController do
  use MyAppWeb, :controller
  def index(conn, %{"kind" => kind}) do
    render(conn, :index, filter: String.to_atom(kind))
  end
end

defmodule MyAppWeb.OrderController do
  use MyAppWeb, :controller
  def index(conn, params) do
    ord = String.to_atom(params["order"] || "desc")
    render(conn, :index, order: ord)
  end
end

defmodule MyAppWeb.TagController do
  use MyAppWeb, :controller
  def show(conn, %{"tag" => tag}) do
    key = :"tag_#{tag}"
    render(conn, :show, tag: key)
  end
end
```

Write all three corrected modules into a single file `lib/my_app_web/controllers/filters.ex`. Use allowlist maps.
tier_rationale: Three sibling vulnerabilities in different controllers. Requires consistent pattern across all of them, not a one-off fix.
